title: Planning and Decision-Making Process
description: Conroe ISD uses a structured planning and decision-making process to involve teachers, staff, parents, and community members in shaping district goals and priorities. Shared decision-making committees at both the campus and district level give stakeholders a voice in areas like instruction, budgeting, and school improvement. Community members who want to understand how key decisions are made — or who are interested in participating — can look to this policy for guidance on how that process works.
description_local: This policy governs how the school district develops and approves its annual District Improvement Plan (DIP), a required document that outlines the district's goals, strategies, and priorities for student achievement and school operations. It applies to the entire school district, including school board members, administrators, staff, and the broader community who have a role in shaping or are affected by district-wide decisions. Parents and community members should care about this policy because the improvement plan directly influences how resources are allocated, what educational priorities are set, and how the district plans to support student success in the coming school year.
